_note: CLOAK EVADE TEST (G4). Offline RE inferred +0x49 = accessory/cloak physical evade (aggregators take max(+0x49, shield +0x4A); struct map's unmapped 'cloak evasion' field). The evade-type +0x1C0 is VM-produced, so this tests whether setting +0x49 high (others 0) makes the VM emit evadeType=0x01 (cloak) - input-control, same mechanism as the proven class/parry/block bytes. Broadcast (TargetCharId=-1 + sweep=32) like the proven evade test: +0x49=100, all other evade bytes (46/47/48/4A/4B/4C/4D/4E)=0. RUN: relaunch, ATTACK any unit (e.g. Ramza) FROM THE FRONT with a physical melee a few times. OBSERVE: the dodge animation + the [SELECTOR-PROBE] evadeType in the log. PASS = evadeType=0x01 (cloak) -> +0x49 is the cloak source and 0x01 is input-selectable. If it shows 0x03 (shield) or 0x00 (hit) instead, +0x49 is NOT the cloak driver -> we fall back to output-control (selector 0x205210 forcing +0x1C0=0x01, already a proven mechanism). BONUS determinism check (G6): this profile also confirms 'all evade bytes except 49 = 0' still allows hits when 49 is irrelevant. Safe: struct writes only.
